description_long: This object is a cluster of five large, blue-gray mushrooms growing closely together from a single, thick base. The caps are broad and funnel-shaped, with deeply wrinkled and radiating gill patterns visible on their undersides. The surface of the caps appears textured and slightly rough, with subtle color variations from pale blue to darker grayish-blue, giving a natural, organic look. The stems are thick, merging at the base, and have a rugged, uneven surface that mimics the appearance of real mushroom stalks. The overall form is irregular, with each cap slightly overlapping or leaning against its neighbors, creating a dense, bushy cluster. This mushroom cluster would likely be found in shaded, moist environments such as urban parks or landscaped green spaces.
description: A dense cluster of five large, blue-gray mushrooms with wrinkled, funnel-shaped caps and thick, rugged stems. The caps display deep gill patterns and subtle color variations, giving a natural, organic appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the cluster's five caps, with the central cap most prominent and deep gill patterns visible.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the overlapping caps and the pronounced wrinkling on the cap surfaces, with the leftmost cap more visible.
description_view_2: The central cap is most forward, with the gills and the merging of the stems at the base clearly visible.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the rightmost caps and the dense, bushy arrangement of the cluster.
